ocr.text: The telecommunications sector within the Asia-Pacific region has witnessed significant fluctuations in roaming revenue trends,
particularly as a result of evolving consumer behavior and regulatory changes. As we analyze the data from Vodafone Group, a
leading player in this market, we can observe a gradual recovery in roaming revenues following the disruptions caused by the
Year  Revenue(CNY)  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)  TotalCustomers  RoamingUsers  AverageRevenue/User  GrowthRate(%)  ARPU(CNY)  PeakMonth  LowestMonth  TotalGrowth
The year 2022 marked a notable increase in roaming revenue, with the figure reaching 1750 CNY million, reflecting a strong
rebound from previous years. This resurgence is indicative of the growing demand for international travel and communication
services as borders reopened and consumer confidence returned. Furthermore, the data highlights that in 2020, the roaming
revenue was recorded at 1300 CNY million, illustrating how the sector has navigated through challenging times. In parallel, the
table provides important insights into customer engagement metrics, including the number of active roaming users, which stood
at 58M in July and saw a slight increase to 55M by January of the following year. These metrics not only underscore the
increasing adoption of roaming services but also signal a shift in consumer preferences towards more connected travel
experiences. As the telecommunications landscape continues to evolve, these trends will be vital for stakeholders in
understanding market dynamics and strategizing for future growth in the Asia-Pacific region.
